title: hypericum tetrapterum subsp. corsicum
intro: Hypericum tetrapterum subsp. corsicum, also known as Corsican St. John's Wort, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the Hypericum genus, characterized by its yellow flowers and four-branched leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. It is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and treating wounds and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
